data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF Stock In The Last 10 Years
data.description:
Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (NASDAQ:VONG) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 3.48% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 15.81%. Currently, Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF has a market capitalization of $37.01 billion.

Buying $1000 In VONG: If an investor had bought $1000 of VONG stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $4,368.53 today based on a price of $110.75 for VONG at the time of writing.

Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF's Performance Over Last 10 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

data.title: What's Going On With Sky Quarry Stock Monday?
data.description:
Nasdaq futures are up 0.40% while S&P 500 futures have gained 0.10%.

Nevada Supply Talks Drive Momentum

Macro Headwinds and Oil Prices

Company Context

Sky Quarry is a development-stage oil-producing and refining company focused on recycling waste asphalt shingles and remediating oil-saturated soils.

The idea is to turn hard-to-dispose waste into refined crude oil products, which can reduce landfill use and lessen reliance on virgin crude sources.

Operationally, the company reports a single segment—refined crude oil—and produces products like Diesel, Liquid Asphalt, Vacuum Gas Oil (VGO), and Naphtha.

For investors, that means the story tends to hinge on execution: scaling the recycling/refining process, product demand, and the economics of converting waste streams into sellable fuels and industrial inputs.

SKYQ Price Action: Sky Quarry shares were down 5.94% at $4.79 at the time of publication on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Mobix Labs Stock Rallies On $3.2M Order Tied To TSA Systems
data.description:
• Mobix Labs shares are powering higher. Why is MOBX stock surging?

Order Expands Program Activity

Mobix Labs said the new order brings total program activity to more than $6 million, expanding its role in a widely deployed airport security platform.

The company expects to recognize revenue from the order over the next three quarters, with shipments scheduled through Dec. 31, 2026.

Technology Used In Daily Airport Screening

Mobix Labs supplies internal components used in full-body screening systems that help identify concealed items at airport security checkpoints.

The company said its technology is deployed in systems used by millions of passengers daily at major U.S. airports, supporting high-throughput screening environments where accuracy, speed and reliability are critical.

Positioned In Ongoing Security Infrastructure Demand

Mobix Labs noted that airport security systems operate under constant use and require ongoing maintenance, upgrades and replacement.

The company said its integration into these platforms creates the potential for continued demand as deployments remain active and expand over time.

Mobix Labs added that it believes it is well positioned to participate in additional opportunities tied to advanced screening systems and related infrastructure.

Mobix Labs Shares Trade Higher

MOBX Price Action: At the time of publication, Mobix shares are trading 2.77% higher at 37 cents, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

This illustration was generated using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.

data.title: Why SanDisk Stock Is Rising Monday On AI Memory Buzz
data.description:
That product launch matters for flow-driven traders because the Roundhill Memory ETF (BATS:DRAM) lists Sandisk at a 4.66% weight, alongside Samsung at 25% and Micron at 24%.

Sandisk's move also comes after a volatile memory tape tied to Alphabet's TurboQuant narrative, which initially spooked investors with claims it could cut memory requirements by 6x.

That earlier shakeout set up a rebound bid in names like Sandisk as the market reassessed whether efficiency"actually reduces total memory demand.

  • SanDisk stock is among today’s top performers. Why are SNDK shares rallying?

New DRAM ETF Adds Fresh Attention To AI Memory Names

Roundhill recently launched the Roundhill Memory ETF, a concentrated fund built around the idea that memory chips are becoming a "key constraint" for AI as training and inference workloads scale. The ETF's focus list highlights memory suppliers including Micron, Samsung, and Sandisk, tying the stock to a fresh product-driven attention cycle in semiconductors.

The broader market is trading in a risk-on posture today, with the Russell 2000 up 0.27%, the Nasdaq up 0.32%, and the S&P 500 up 0.12%.

Sandisk Holds Near 52-Week High As Traders Watch Resistance

Technical Analysis At $722.20, Sandisk is holding near the top of its 52-week range, which is consistent with buyers defending a strong uptrend. With moving averages and RSI/MACD readings listed as N/A, the chart read leans more on range positioning and prior turning points than on indicator confirmation.

The stock's 12-month gain of 2179.51% is an extreme run and is backward-looking, but it helps explain why pullbacks can be sharp when momentum cools. The last 52-week high was set on 2026-03-20 at $777.60, so price is still trading close enough that traders often watch for either a retest or a failed rally.

  • Key Resistance: $777.50 — near the prior peak area where rallies have recently stalled.

Sandisk's NAND Footprint Keeps It In The AI Memory Trade

Sandisk is one of the five largest suppliers of NAND flash memory semiconductors globally, and it's vertically integrated through manufacturing sites across Japan via a joint-venture framework with Kioxia. It then repackages much of that flash into SSDs used in consumer devices, external storage, and cloud storage.

That matters for today's ETF-driven attention because "memory" in the AI stack isn't just one product category—investors often basket NAND and DRAM exposure together when they're positioning for AI infrastructure demand. Sandisk's 2025 spin-off from Western Digital also means the stock can trade more "pure-play" on memory-cycle expectations than it did inside a larger HDD/flash mix.

April 30 Earnings Report Becomes Sandisk's Next Big Catalyst

Following last quarter’s results, investors are now tracking the path toward the next reporting date on April 30.

  • EPS Estimate: $13.72 (Up from a loss of 30 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $4.59 Billion (Up from $1.70 Billion YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $574.67. Recent analyst moves include:

  • B of A Securities: Buy (Raises Target to $900.00) (March 23)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Raises Target to $875.00) (March 19)
  • KGI Securities: Initiated with Outperform (Target $992.00) (March 18)

SNDK Shares Edge Higher Monday

SNDK Price Action: SanDisk shares were up 3.84% at $728.51 at the time of publication on Monday. The stock is approaching its 52-week high of $777.60, according to Benzinga Pro data.
